setupGuide.notes: AMS2's Jerez 1988 is the same original layout Jerez opened with; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.

track.realVenue: Autodromo Internazionale Enzo e Dino Ferrari

setupGuide.notes: AMS2's Imola 1988 is the same pre-1995 layout driven in 1986; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.
No 1986 Spa layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the 1993 layout.
AMS2's historic Montreal is the 1988-spec layout; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.
Placeholder for Detroit Street Circuit: 63 laps / 253.4 km reproduced as 80 laps of Long Beach. Long Beach hosted the United States GP West 1976-1983, the era's US street-race character.
Placeholder for Circuit Paul Ricard: 80 laps / 305 km reproduced as 73 laps of Le Mans Circuit Bugatti. Keeps the French GP at a French Grand Prix venue.
AMS2's Brands Hatch GP is the modern layout of the same venue; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.
AMS2's Hockenheim 1988 is the same configuration driven in 1986; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.
AMS2 ships the 2025 Hungaroring; the venue is real and largely unchanged in character since 1986, so the historical lap count is kept.
The 1986 Austrian GP ran the Österreichring, AMS2's Spielberg Historic, the 1977 layout with the Hella-Licht chicane still driven in 1986; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.
No 1986 Monza in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-closest 1991 layout.
AMS2's Cascais 1988 (Estoril) is the same layout driven in 1986; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.
Placeholder for Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez: 68 laps / 300.6 km reproduced as 70 laps of Interlagos 1991. Interlagos 1991 keeps the period Latin-American venue character.
